title: Ultra-spicy Canadian Tourtière Poutine
description: A spicy twist on the classic Canadian Tourtière dish, slow-cooked to perfection and served with creamy coconut milk, crispy fries, and a side of hangover-curing magic.
instructions: In a large Dutch oven, cook the bacon over medium-high heat until crispy. Remove the bacon from the pot and set aside.
Add the diced onion, carrots, and celery to the pot and cook until the vegetables are tender, about 10 minutes.
Add the ground beef to the pot, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Add the red pepper flakes, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper to the pot and stir to combine.
Add the flour to the pot and stir to combine. Cook for 1-2 minutes.
Add 1 cup of beef broth to the pot and stir to combine. Bring the mixture to a simmer.
Transfer the pot to the preheated oven and cook, covered, for 2 1/2 hours.
Remove the pot from the oven and stir in the coconut cream. Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the tourtière over crispy fries, garnished with chopped cilantro.
Garnish with additional red pepper flakes, if desired.
